response_turn1:
Thank you for the retrieved text. Here is my assessment:

LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 62
REASON: While the document uses accurate GDPR legal references (Articles 4(1), 5(2), 9, 11, 35, Chapters III and V) and the substantive content is broadly consistent with established EDPB positions on pseudonymisation, the actual EDPB work programme and published guidelines list cannot confirm that "Guidelines 01/2025 on Pseudonymisation" is a real adopted document — the text is unusually short and stripped of the structural features (version history, page numbering, executive summary, annex, legal basis recitals, and formal adoption language) typical of genuine EDPB guideline documents, suggesting it may be a plausible but synthetic or heavily paraphrased rendering rather than the authentic full text.
